meaning: adj.(浜嬩欢銆佽涓虹瓑)鍐冲畾鎬х殑
examples: Although not dispositive, the student body is more than 99 percent white.
You must ensure that each of your CROs has the authority to make dispositive resolution of complaints on behalf of the carrier.
The age guidelines provided by librarians, education professionals, and publishers may be dispositive for determining the intended audience.
So cost-benefit analysis is rarely dispositive, but to say that costs are irrelevant is just misguided.
One potentially dispositive question is what mix of Republicans and Democrats will show up this election.
